title: dianthus flahaultii
intro: Dianthus flahaultii, also known as the alpine pink or Flahault's pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Dianthus flahaultii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Dianthus flahaultii can be made into infusions, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds.
